description_long: This object consists of two adjacent wooden picnic tables, each constructed from weathered planks with visible signs of wear and paint chipping. One table is painted a faded green, while the other is a distressed white, both showing exposed wood beneath the paint. Each table features a rectangular tabletop supported by sturdy A-frame legs and crossbeams, with attached benches running parallel to the tabletop on either side. The benches and tabletops are made from the same type of wood, and the tables are joined closely but remain distinct units. The surfaces are flat and broad, suitable for seating and placing items, and the overall build suggests durability for outdoor use. The rustic, utilitarian design is typical of public park or recreational area furniture.
description: Two weathered wooden picnic tables, one green and one white, each with attached benches and sturdy A-frame supports, showing signs of outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both tables side by side, highlighting the green table on the left and the white table on the right, with visible bench and tabletop surfaces.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the length of the benches and the thickness of the planks, with the green table behind the white table.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the white table now on the left and the green table on the right, showing the opposite sides of the benches and tabletops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, with the green table in front and the white table behind, again highlighting the benches and support structure.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: Each tabletop is supported by two A-frame legs connected by horizontal crossbeams and diagonal braces, providing stability and strength.
